inetres/IZ_PolicyActiveScripting_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether script code on pages in the zone is run.

If you enable this policy setting, script code on pages in the zone can run automatically. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow script code on pages in the zone to run.

If you disable this policy setting, script code on pages in the zone is prevented from running.

If you do not configure this policy setting, script code on pages in the zone is prevent...
inetres/IZ_PolicyActiveScripting_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowMETAREFRESH_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether a user's browser can be redirected to another Web page if the author of the Web page uses the Meta Refresh setting (tag) to redirect browsers to another Web page.

If you enable this policy setting, a user's browser that loads a page containing an active Meta Refresh setting can be redirected to another Web page.

If you disable this policy setting, a user's browser that loads a page containing an active Meta Refresh setting cannot be redirecte...
inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowMETAREFRESH_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowPasteViaScript_7.Friendly: Allow cut, copy or paste operations from the clipboard via script
inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowPasteViaScript_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether scripts can perform a clipboard operation (for example, cut, copy, and paste) in a specified region.

If you enable this policy setting, a script can perform a clipboard operation.

If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried as to whether to perform clipboard operations.

If you disable this policy setting, a script cannot perform a clipboard operation.

If you do not configure this policy setting, a script cannot perform a cl...
inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowPasteViaScript_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyBinaryBehaviors_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage dynamic binary and script behaviors: components that encapsulate specific functionality for HTML elements to which they were attached.

If you enable this policy setting, binary and script behaviors are available. If you select Administrator approved in the drop-down box, only behaviors listed in the Admin-approved Behaviors under Binary Behaviors Security Restriction policy are available.

If you disable this policy setting, binary and script behavior...
inetres/IZ_PolicyBinaryBehaviors_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyBlockPopupWindows_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether unwanted pop-up windows appear. Pop-up windows that are opened when the end user clicks a link are not blocked.

If you enable this policy setting, most unwanted pop-up windows are prevented from appearing.

If you disable this policy setting, pop-up windows are not prevented from appearing.

If you do not configure this policy setting, most unwanted pop-up windows are prevented from appearing.
inetres/IZ_PolicyBlockPopupWindows_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDisplayMixedContent_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users can display nonsecure items and manage whether users receive a security information message to display pages containing both secure and nonsecure items.

If you enable this policy setting, and the drop-down box is set to Enable, the user does not receive a security information message (This page contains both secure and nonsecure items. Do you want to display the nonsecure items?) and nonsecure content can be displayed.

If the drop-down ...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDisplayMixedContent_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDownloadSignedActiveX_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users may download signed ActiveX controls from a page in the zone.

If you enable this policy, users can download signed controls without user intervention. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried whether to download controls signed by publishers who aren't trusted. Code signed by trusted publishers is silently downloaded.

If you disable the policy setting, signed controls cannot be downloaded.

If you do not configure th...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDownloadSignedActiveX_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDownloadUnsignedActiveX.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users may download unsigned ActiveX controls from the zone. Such code is potentially harmful, especially when coming from an untrusted zone.

If you enable this policy setting, users can run unsigned controls without user intervention. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow the unsigned control to run.

If you disable this policy setting, users cannot run unsigned controls.

If you do not configu...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDownloadUnsignedActiveX.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDropOrPasteFiles_7.Friendly: Allow drag and drop or copy and paste files
inetres/IZ_PolicyDropOrPasteFiles_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users can drag files or copy and paste files from a source within the zone.

If you enable this policy setting, users can drag files or copy and paste files from this zone automatically. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to drag or copy files from this zone.

If you disable this policy setting, users are prevented from dragging files or copying and pasting files from this zone.

If you do not configu...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDropOrPasteFiles_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyRenderLegacyFilters_7.Desc:
This policy setting specifies whether Internet Explorer renders legacy visual filters in this zone.

If you enable this policy setting, you can control whether or not Internet Explorer renders legacy filters by selecting Enable, or Disable, under Options in Group Policy Editor.

If you disable, or do not configure this policy setting, users can choose whether or not to render filters in this zone. Users can change this setting on the Security tab of the Internet Options dialog box. Filters ar...
inetres/IZ_PolicyRenderLegacyFilters_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyFileDownload_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether file downloads are permitted from the zone. This option is determined by the zone of the page with the link causing the download, not the zone from which the file is delivered.

If you enable this policy setting, files can be downloaded from the zone.

If you disable this policy setting, files are prevented from being downloaded from the zone.

 If you do not configure this policy setting, files are prevented from being downloaded from the zone.
inetres/IZ_PolicyFileDownload_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyFontDownload_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether pages of the zone may download HTML fonts.

If you enable this policy setting, HTML fonts can be downloaded automatically. If you enable this policy setting and Prompt is selected in the drop-down box, users are queried whether to allow HTML fonts to download.

If you disable this policy setting, HTML fonts are prevented from downloading.

If you do not configure this policy setting, users are queried whether to allow HTML fonts to download.
inetres/IZ_PolicyFontDownload_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyInstallDesktopItems_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users can install Active Desktop items from this zone. The settings for this option are: If you enable this policy setting, users can install desktop items from this zone automatically.

If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to install desktop items from this zone.

If you disable this policy setting, users are prevented from installing desktop items from this zone.

If you do not configure this policy s...
inetres/IZ_PolicyInstallDesktopItems_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyJavaPermissions_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage permissions for Java applets.

If you enable this policy setting, you can choose options from the drop-down box. Custom, to control permissions settings individually.

Low Safety enables applets to perform all operations.

Medium Safety enables applets to run in their sandbox (an area in memory outside of which the program cannot make calls), plus capabilities like scratch space (a safe and secure storage area on the client computer) and user-controlle...
inetres/IZ_PolicyJavaPermissions_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyLaunchAppsAndFilesInIFR.Friendly: Launching applications and files in an IFRAME
inetres/IZ_PolicyLaunchAppsAndFilesInIFR.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether applications may be run and files may be downloaded from an IFRAME reference in the HTML of the pages in this zone.

If you enable this policy setting, users can run applications and download files from IFRAMEs on the pages in this zone without user intervention. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to run applications and download files from IFRAMEs on the pages in this zone.

If you disable this polic...
inetres/IZ_PolicyLaunchAppsAndFilesInIFR.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyLogon_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage settings for logon options.

If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from the following logon options.

Anonymous logon to disable HTTP authentication and use the guest account only for the Common Internet File System (CIFS) protocol.

Prompt for user name and password to query users for user IDs and passwords. After a user is queried, these values can be used silently for the remainder of the session.

Automatic logon only in Intranet zone t...

EnumValues.65536: Prompt for user name and password
EnumValues.0: Automatic logon with current username and password
EnumValues.131072: Automatic logon only in Intranet zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyMimeSniffingURLaction_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage MIME sniffing for file promotion from one type to another based on a MIME sniff. A MIME sniff is the recognition by Internet Explorer of the file type based on a bit signature.

If you enable this policy setting, the MIME Sniffing Safety Feature will not apply in this zone. The security zone will run without the added layer of security provided by this feature.

If you disable this policy setting, the actions that may be harmful cannot run; this Intern...
inetres/IZ_PolicyMimeSniffingURLaction_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsWi.Friendly: Enable dragging of content from different domains within a window
inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsWi.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to set options for dragging content from one domain to a different domain when the source and destination are in the same window.

If you enable this policy setting and click Enable, users can drag content from one domain to a different domain when the source and destination are in the same window. Users cannot change this setting.

If you enable this policy setting and click Disable, users cannot drag content from one domain to a different domain when the sourc...

inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsAc.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to set options for dragging content from one domain to a different domain when the source and destination are in different windows.

If you enable this policy setting and click Enable, users can drag content from one domain to a different domain when the source and destination are in different windows. Users cannot change this setting.

If you enable this policy setting and click Disable, users cannot drag content from one domain to a different domain when both ...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsAc.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyNavigateSubframesAcross.Desc:

If you enable this policy setting, users can open additional windows and frames from other domains and access applications from other domains. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried whether to allow additional windows and frames to access applications from other domains.

If you disable this policy setting, users cannot open other windows and f...
inetres/IZ_PolicyNavigateSubframesAcross.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyNetworkProtocolLockdown.Friendly: Allow active content over restricted protocols to access my computer
inetres/IZ_PolicyNetworkProtocolLockdown.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether a resource hosted on an admin-restricted protocol in the Trusted Sites Zone can run active content such as script, ActiveX, Java and Binary Behaviors. The list of restricted protocols may be set in the Trusted Sites Zone Restricted Protocols section under Network Protocol Lockdown policy.

If you enable this policy setting, no Trusted Sites Zone content accessed is affected, even for protocols on the restricted list. If you select Prompt from t...

This policy setting allows you to manage whether users are prompted to select a certificate when no certificate or only one certificate exists.

If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer does not prompt users with a "Client Authentication" message when they connect to a Web site that has no certificate or only one certificate.

If you disable this policy setting, Internet Explorer prompts users with a "Client Authentication" message when they connect to a Web site that has no certi...
inetres/IZ_PolicyNoPromptForOneOrNoClien.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyNotificationBarActiveXU.Desc:
This policy setting manages whether users will be automatically prompted for ActiveX control installations.

If you enable this policy setting, users will receive a prompt when a site instantiates an ActiveX control they do not have installed.

If you disable this policy setting, ActiveX control installations will be blocked using the Notification bar. Users can click on the Notification bar to allow the ActiveX control prompt.

If you do not configure this policy setting, ActiveX control ins...
inetres/IZ_PolicyNotificationBarActiveXU.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyNotificationBarDownload.Desc:
This policy setting determines whether users will be prompted for non user-initiated file downloads. Regardless of this setting, users will receive file download dialogs for user-initiated downloads.

If you enable this setting, users will receive a file download dialog for automatic download attempts.

If you disable or do not configure this setting, file downloads that are not user-initiated will be blocked, and users will see the Notification bar instead of the file download dialog. Users ...

inetres/IZ_PolicyRunActiveXControls_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether ActiveX controls and plug-ins can be run on pages from the specified zone.

If you enable this policy setting, controls and plug-ins can run without user intervention.

If you selected Prompt in the drop-down box, users are asked to choose whether to allow the controls or plug-in to run.

If you disable this policy setting, controls and plug-ins are prevented from running.

If you do not configure this policy setting, controls and plug-ins are ...

inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptActiveXMarkedSafe.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether an ActiveX control marked safe for scripting can interact with a script.

If you enable this policy setting, script interaction can occur automatically without user intervention.

If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow script interaction.

If you disable this policy setting, script interaction is prevented from occurring.

If you do not configure this policy setting, script interaction is prevent...

inetres/IZ_PolicyAntiMalwareCheckingOfAc.Desc:
This policy setting determines whether Internet Explorer runs antimalware programs against ActiveX controls, to check if they're safe to load on pages.

If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer won't check with your antimalware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control.

If you disable this policy setting, Internet Explorer always checks with your antimalware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control.

inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptActiveXNotMarkedS.Friendly: Initialize and script ActiveX controls not marked as safe
inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptActiveXNotMarkedS.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage ActiveX controls not marked as safe.

If you enable this policy setting, ActiveX controls are run, loaded with parameters, and scripted without setting object safety for untrusted data or scripts. This setting is not recommended, except for secure and administered zones. This setting causes both unsafe and safe controls to be initialized and scripted, ignoring the Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting option.

inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptingOfJavaApplets_.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether applets are exposed to scripts within the zone.

If you enable this policy setting, scripts can access applets automatically without user intervention.

If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow scripts to access applets.

If you disable this policy setting, scripts are prevented from accessing applets.

If you do not configure this policy setting, scripts are prevented from accessing applets.

This policy setting allows you to manage whether .NET Framework components that are signed with Authenticode can be executed from Internet Explorer. These components include managed controls referenced from an object tag and managed executables referenced from a link.

If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer will execute signed managed components. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, Internet Explorer will prompt the user to determine whether to execute signed managed compo...
inetres/IZ_PolicySignedFrameworkComponen.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicySoftwareChannelPermissi.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage software channel permissions.

If you enable this policy setting, you can choose the following options from the drop-down box.

Low safety to allow users to be notified of software updates by e-mail, software packages to be automatically downloaded to users' computers, and software packages to be automatically installed on users' computers.

Medium safety to allow users to be notified of software updates by e-mail and software packages to be automatica...
inetres/IZ_PolicySoftwareChannelPermissi.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicySubmitNonencryptedFormD.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether data on HTML forms on pages in the zone may be submitted. Forms sent with SSL (Secure Sockets Layer) encryption are always allowed; this setting only affects non-SSL form data submission.

If you enable this policy setting, information using HTML forms on pages in this zone can be submitted automatically. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow information using HTML forms on pages in this zone to...
inetres/IZ_PolicySubmitNonencryptedFormD.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyUnsignedFrameworkCompon.Friendly: Run .NET Framework-reliant components not signed with Authenticode
inetres/IZ_PolicyUnsignedFrameworkCompon.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether .NET Framework components that are not signed with Authenticode can be executed from Internet Explorer. These components include managed controls referenced from an object tag and managed executables referenced from a link.

If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer will execute unsigned managed components. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, Internet Explorer will prompt the user to determine whether to execute unsigned manag...
inetres/IZ_PolicyUnsignedFrameworkCompon.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyUserdataPersistence_7.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage the preservation of information in the browser's history, in favorites, in an XML store, or directly within a Web page saved to disk. When a user returns to a persisted page, the state of the page can be restored if this policy setting is appropriately configured.

If you enable this policy setting, users can preserve information in the browser's history, in favorites, in an XML store, or directly within a Web page saved to disk.

If you disable this p...
inetres/IZ_PolicyUserdataPersistence_7.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyWindowsRestrictionsURLa.Friendly: Allow script-initiated windows without size or position constraints
inetres/IZ_PolicyWindowsRestrictionsURLa.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage restrictions on script-initiated pop-up windows and windows that include the title and status bars.

If you enable this policy setting, Windows Restrictions security will not apply in this zone. The security zone runs without the added layer of security provided by this feature.

If you disable this policy setting, the possible harmful actions contained in script-initiated pop-up windows and windows that include the title and status bars cannot be run....

inetres/IZ_PolicyZoneElevationURLaction_.Friendly: Web sites in less privileged Web content zones can navigate into this zone
inetres/IZ_PolicyZoneElevationURLaction_.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether Web sites from less privileged zones, such as Internet sites, can navigate into this zone.

If you enable this policy setting, Web sites from less privileged zones can open new windows in, or navigate into, this zone. The security zone will run without the added layer of security that is provided by the Protection from Zone Elevation security feature. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, a warning is issued to the user that potentially ri...

This policy setting allows you to manage whether VBScript can be run on pages from the specified zone in Internet Explorer.

If you selected Enable in the drop-down box, VBScript can run without user intervention.

If you selected Prompt in the drop-down box, users are asked to choose whether to allow VBScript to run.

If you selected Disable in the drop-down box, VBScript is prevented from running.

If you do not configure or disable this policy setting, VBScript is prevented from running.

inetres/IZ_Policy_AddressStatusBar_8.Friendly: Allow websites to open windows without status bar or Address bar
inetres/IZ_Policy_AddressStatusBar_8.Desc:
This policy setting controls whether websites can open new Internet Explorer windows that have no status bar or Address bar.

If you enable this policy setting, websites can open new Internet Explorer windows that have no status bar or Address bar.

If you disable this policy setting, websites cannot open new Internet Explorer windows that have no status bar or Address bar.

If you do not configure this policy setting, the user can choose whether websites can open new Internet Explorer Window...

This policy setting allows the playing of video and animation through older media players in specified zones. Video and animation playback through the object tag may still be allowed, because this involves external controls or media players.

The dynsrc attribute on the img tag specifies an older media player. Also, as of Internet Explorer 8, this policy setting controls HTML+TIME media elements that refer to audio and video files.

inetres/IZ_Policy_AllowDynsrcPlayback_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_AllowScriptlets_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether the user can run scriptlets.

If you enable this policy setting, the user can run scriptlets.

If you disable this policy setting, the user cannot run scriptlets.

If you do not configure this policy setting, the user can enable or disable scriptlets.

inetres/IZ_Policy_FirstRunOptIn_8.Desc:
This policy setting controls the first-run response that the user sees on a zone-by-zone basis. When the user encounters a new control that has not previously run in Internet Explorer, he or she may be prompted to approve the control. This policy setting determines whether the user is prompted.

If you enable this policy setting, the first-run prompt is turned off in the corresponding zone.

If you disable this policy setting, the first-run prompt is turned on in the corresponding zone.

If y...
inetres/IZ_Policy_FirstRunOptIn_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_LocalPathForUpload_8.Friendly: Include local path when user is uploading files to a server
inetres/IZ_Policy_LocalPathForUpload_8.Desc:
This policy setting controls whether or not local path information is sent when the user is uploading a file via an HTML form. If the local path information is sent, some information may be unintentionally revealed to the server. For instance, files sent from the user's desktop may contain the user name as a part of the path.

If you enable this policy setting, path information is sent when the user is uploading a file via an HTML form.

If you disable this policy setting, path information is...
inetres/IZ_Policy_LocalPathForUpload_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_Phishing_8.Desc:
This policy setting controls whether SmartScreen Filter scans pages in this zone for malicious content.

If you enable this policy setting, SmartScreen Filter scans pages in this zone for malicious content.

If you disable this policy setting, SmartScreen Filter does not scan pages in this zone for malicious content.

If you do not configure this policy setting, the user can choose whether SmartScreen Filter scans pages in this zone for malicious content.

Note: In Internet Explorer 7, this p...
inetres/IZ_Policy_Phishing_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_ScriptPrompt_8.Friendly: Allow websites to prompt for information by using scripted windows
inetres/IZ_Policy_ScriptPrompt_8.Desc:
This policy setting determines whether scripted windows are automatically displayed.

If you enable this policy setting, scripted windows are displayed.

If you disable this policy setting, the user must choose to display any scripted windows by using the Notification bar.

If you do not configure this policy setting, the user can enable or disable the Notification bar behavior.
inetres/IZ_Policy_ScriptPrompt_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_ScriptStatusBar_8.Friendly: Allow updates to status bar via script
inetres/IZ_Policy_ScriptStatusBar_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether script is allowed to update the status bar within the zone.

If you enable this policy setting, script is allowed to update the status bar.

If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, script is not allowed to update the status bar.
inetres/IZ_Policy_ScriptStatusBar_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_TurnOnProtectedMode_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to turn on Protected Mode. Protected Mode helps protect Internet Explorer from exploited vulnerabilities by reducing the locations that Internet Explorer can write to in the registry and the file system.

If you enable this policy setting, Protected Mode is turned on. The user cannot turn off Protected Mode.

If you disable this policy setting, Protected Mode is turned off. The user cannot turn on Protected Mode.

If you do not configure this policy setting, the...
inetres/IZ_Policy_TurnOnProtectedMode_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_UnsafeFiles_8.Friendly: Show security warning for potentially unsafe files
inetres/IZ_Policy_UnsafeFiles_8.Desc:
This policy setting controls whether or not the "Open File - Security Warning" message appears when the user tries to open executable files or other potentially unsafe files (from an intranet file share by using File Explorer, for example).

If you enable this policy setting and set the drop-down box to Enable, these files open without a security warning. If you set the drop-down box to Prompt, a security warning appears before the files open.

If you disable this policy setting, these files ...
inetres/IZ_Policy_UnsafeFiles_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_WebBrowserApps_8.Friendly: Allow loading of XAML Browser Applications
inetres/IZ_Policy_WebBrowserApps_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage the loading of XAML Browser Applications (XBAPs). These are browser-hosted, ClickOnce-deployed applications built via WinFX. These applications run in a security sandbox and take advantage of the Windows Presentation Foundation platform for the web.

If you enable this policy setting and set the drop-down box to Enable, XBAPs are automatically loaded inside Internet Explorer. The user cannot change this behavior. If you set the drop-down box to Prompt,...
inetres/IZ_Policy_WebBrowserApps_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_WebBrowserControl_8.Friendly: Allow scripting of Internet Explorer WebBrowser controls
inetres/IZ_Policy_WebBrowserControl_8.Desc:
This policy setting determines whether a page can control embedded WebBrowser controls via script.

If you enable this policy setting, script access to the WebBrowser control is allowed.

If you disable this policy setting, script access to the WebBrowser control is not allowed.

If you do not configure this policy setting, the user can enable or disable script access to the WebBrowser control. By default, script access to the WebBrowser control is allowed only in the Local Machine and Intran...
inetres/IZ_Policy_WebBrowserControl_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_WinFXRuntimeComponent_.Desc:
This policy setting prevents the user's computer from starting Microsoft .NET Framework Setup when the user is browsing to .NET Framework content in Internet Explorer. The .NET Framework is the next-generation platform for Windows. It uses the common language runtime and incorporates support from multiple developer tools. It includes the new managed code APIs for Windows.

If you enable this policy setting, .NET Framework Setup is turned off. The user cannot change this behavior.

If you disa...
inetres/IZ_Policy_WinFXRuntimeComponent_.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_XAML_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage the loading of Extensible Application Markup Language (XAML) files. XAML is an XML-based declarative markup language commonly used for creating rich user interfaces and graphics that take advantage of the Windows Presentation Foundation.

If you enable this policy setting and set the drop-down box to Enable, XAML files are automatically loaded inside Internet Explorer. The user cannot change this behavior. If you set the drop-down box to Prompt, the us...
inetres/IZ_Policy_XAML_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_Policy_XPS_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage the loading of XPS files. These files contain a fixed-layout representation of paginated content and are portable across platforms, devices, and applications.

If you enable this policy setting and set the drop-down box to Enable, XPS files are automatically loaded inside Internet Explorer. The user cannot change this behavior. If you set the drop-down box to Prompt, the user is prompted for loading XPS files.

If you disable this policy setting, XPS f...
inetres/IZ_Policy_XPS_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyAccessDataSourcesAcross.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether Internet Explorer can access data from another security zone using the Microsoft XML Parser (MSXML) or ActiveX Data Objects (ADO).

If you enable this policy setting, users can load a page in the zone that uses MSXML or ADO to access data from another site in the zone. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow a page to be loaded in the zone that uses MSXML or ADO to access data from another site in...
inetres/IZ_PolicyAccessDataSourcesAcross.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyActiveScripting_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether script code on pages in the zone is run.

If you enable this policy setting, script code on pages in the zone can run automatically. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow script code on pages in the zone to run.

If you disable this policy setting, script code on pages in the zone is prevented from running.

If you do not configure this policy setting, script code on pages in the zone is prevent...
inetres/IZ_PolicyActiveScripting_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowMETAREFRESH_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether a user's browser can be redirected to another Web page if the author of the Web page uses the Meta Refresh setting (tag) to redirect browsers to another Web page.

If you enable this policy setting, a user's browser that loads a page containing an active Meta Refresh setting can be redirected to another Web page.

If you disable this policy setting, a user's browser that loads a page containing an active Meta Refresh setting cannot be redirecte...
inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowMETAREFRESH_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowPasteViaScript_8.Friendly: Allow cut, copy or paste operations from the clipboard via script
inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowPasteViaScript_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether scripts can perform a clipboard operation (for example, cut, copy, and paste) in a specified region.

If you enable this policy setting, a script can perform a clipboard operation.

If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried as to whether to perform clipboard operations.

If you disable this policy setting, a script cannot perform a clipboard operation.

If you do not configure this policy setting, a script cannot perform a cl...
inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowPasteViaScript_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyBinaryBehaviors_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage dynamic binary and script behaviors: components that encapsulate specific functionality for HTML elements to which they were attached.

If you enable this policy setting, binary and script behaviors are available. If you select Administrator approved in the drop-down box, only behaviors listed in the Admin-approved Behaviors under Binary Behaviors Security Restriction policy are available.

If you disable this policy setting, binary and script behavior...
inetres/IZ_PolicyBinaryBehaviors_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyBlockPopupWindows_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether unwanted pop-up windows appear. Pop-up windows that are opened when the end user clicks a link are not blocked.

If you enable this policy setting, most unwanted pop-up windows are prevented from appearing.

If you disable this policy setting, pop-up windows are not prevented from appearing.

If you do not configure this policy setting, most unwanted pop-up windows are prevented from appearing.
inetres/IZ_PolicyBlockPopupWindows_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDisplayMixedContent_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users can display nonsecure items and manage whether users receive a security information message to display pages containing both secure and nonsecure items.

If you enable this policy setting, and the drop-down box is set to Enable, the user does not receive a security information message (This page contains both secure and nonsecure items. Do you want to display the nonsecure items?) and nonsecure content can be displayed.

If the drop-down ...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDisplayMixedContent_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDownloadSignedActiveX_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users may download signed ActiveX controls from a page in the zone.

If you enable this policy, users can download signed controls without user intervention. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried whether to download controls signed by publishers who aren't trusted. Code signed by trusted publishers is silently downloaded.

If you disable the policy setting, signed controls cannot be downloaded.

If you do not configure th...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDownloadSignedActiveX_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDownloadUnsignedActiveX.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users may download unsigned ActiveX controls from the zone. Such code is potentially harmful, especially when coming from an untrusted zone.

If you enable this policy setting, users can run unsigned controls without user intervention. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow the unsigned control to run.

If you disable this policy setting, users cannot run unsigned controls.

If you do not configu...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDownloadUnsignedActiveX.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDropOrPasteFiles_8.Friendly: Allow drag and drop or copy and paste files
inetres/IZ_PolicyDropOrPasteFiles_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users can drag files or copy and paste files from a source within the zone.

If you enable this policy setting, users can drag files or copy and paste files from this zone automatically. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to drag or copy files from this zone.

If you disable this policy setting, users are prevented from dragging files or copying and pasting files from this zone.

If you do not configu...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDropOrPasteFiles_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyRenderLegacyFilters_8.Desc:
This policy setting specifies whether Internet Explorer renders legacy visual filters in this zone.

If you enable this policy setting, you can control whether or not Internet Explorer renders legacy filters by selecting Enable, or Disable, under Options in Group Policy Editor.

If you disable, or do not configure this policy setting, users can choose whether or not to render filters in this zone. Users can change this setting on the Security tab of the Internet Options dialog box. Filters ar...
inetres/IZ_PolicyRenderLegacyFilters_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyFileDownload_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether file downloads are permitted from the zone. This option is determined by the zone of the page with the link causing the download, not the zone from which the file is delivered.

If you enable this policy setting, files can be downloaded from the zone.

If you disable this policy setting, files are prevented from being downloaded from the zone.

 If you do not configure this policy setting, files are prevented from being downloaded from the zone.
inetres/IZ_PolicyFileDownload_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyFontDownload_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether pages of the zone may download HTML fonts.

If you enable this policy setting, HTML fonts can be downloaded automatically. If you enable this policy setting and Prompt is selected in the drop-down box, users are queried whether to allow HTML fonts to download.

If you disable this policy setting, HTML fonts are prevented from downloading.

If you do not configure this policy setting, users are queried whether to allow HTML fonts to download.
inetres/IZ_PolicyFontDownload_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyInstallDesktopItems_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users can install Active Desktop items from this zone. The settings for this option are: If you enable this policy setting, users can install desktop items from this zone automatically.

If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to install desktop items from this zone.

If you disable this policy setting, users are prevented from installing desktop items from this zone.

If you do not configure this policy s...
inetres/IZ_PolicyInstallDesktopItems_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyJavaPermissions_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage permissions for Java applets.

If you enable this policy setting, you can choose options from the drop-down box. Custom, to control permissions settings individually.

Low Safety enables applets to perform all operations.

Medium Safety enables applets to run in their sandbox (an area in memory outside of which the program cannot make calls), plus capabilities like scratch space (a safe and secure storage area on the client computer) and user-controlle...
inetres/IZ_PolicyJavaPermissions_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyLaunchAppsAndFilesInIFR.Friendly: Launching applications and files in an IFRAME
inetres/IZ_PolicyLaunchAppsAndFilesInIFR.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether applications may be run and files may be downloaded from an IFRAME reference in the HTML of the pages in this zone.

If you enable this policy setting, users can run applications and download files from IFRAMEs on the pages in this zone without user intervention. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to run applications and download files from IFRAMEs on the pages in this zone.

If you disable this polic...
inetres/IZ_PolicyLaunchAppsAndFilesInIFR.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyLogon_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage settings for logon options.

If you enable this policy setting, you can choose from the following logon options.

Anonymous logon to disable HTTP authentication and use the guest account only for the Common Internet File System (CIFS) protocol.

Prompt for user name and password to query users for user IDs and passwords. After a user is queried, these values can be used silently for the remainder of the session.

Automatic logon only in Intranet zone t...
inetres/IZ_PolicyLogon_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

EnumValues.65536: Prompt for user name and password
EnumValues.0: Automatic logon with current username and password
EnumValues.131072: Automatic logon only in Intranet zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyMimeSniffingURLaction_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage MIME sniffing for file promotion from one type to another based on a MIME sniff. A MIME sniff is the recognition by Internet Explorer of the file type based on a bit signature.

If you enable this policy setting, the MIME Sniffing Safety Feature will not apply in this zone. The security zone will run without the added layer of security provided by this feature.

If you disable this policy setting, the actions that may be harmful cannot run; this Intern...
inetres/IZ_PolicyMimeSniffingURLaction_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsWi.Friendly: Enable dragging of content from different domains within a window
inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsWi.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to set options for dragging content from one domain to a different domain when the source and destination are in the same window.

If you enable this policy setting and click Enable, users can drag content from one domain to a different domain when the source and destination are in the same window. Users cannot change this setting.

If you enable this policy setting and click Disable, users cannot drag content from one domain to a different domain when the sourc...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsWi.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsAc.Friendly: Enable dragging of content from different domains across windows
inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsAc.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to set options for dragging content from one domain to a different domain when the source and destination are in different windows.

If you enable this policy setting and click Enable, users can drag content from one domain to a different domain when the source and destination are in different windows. Users cannot change this setting.

If you enable this policy setting and click Disable, users cannot drag content from one domain to a different domain when both ...
inetres/IZ_PolicyDragDropAcrossDomainsAc.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyNavigateSubframesAcross.Friendly: Navigate windows and frames across different domains
inetres/IZ_PolicyNavigateSubframesAcross.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage the opening of windows and frames and access of applications across different domains.

If you enable this policy setting, users can open additional windows and frames from other domains and access applications from other domains. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried whether to allow additional windows and frames to access applications from other domains.

If you disable this policy setting, users cannot open other windows and f...
inetres/IZ_PolicyNavigateSubframesAcross.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyNoPromptForOneOrNoClien.Friendly: Do not prompt for client certificate selection when no certificates or only one certificate exists.
inetres/IZ_PolicyNoPromptForOneOrNoClien.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether users are prompted to select a certificate when no certificate or only one certificate exists.

If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer does not prompt users with a "Client Authentication" message when they connect to a Web site that has no certificate or only one certificate.

If you disable this policy setting, Internet Explorer prompts users with a "Client Authentication" message when they connect to a Web site that has no certi...
inetres/IZ_PolicyNoPromptForOneOrNoClien.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyNotificationBarActiveXU.Desc:
This policy setting manages whether users will be automatically prompted for ActiveX control installations.

If you enable this policy setting, users will receive a prompt when a site instantiates an ActiveX control they do not have installed.

If you disable this policy setting, ActiveX control installations will be blocked using the Notification bar. Users can click on the Notification bar to allow the ActiveX control prompt.

If you do not configure this policy setting, ActiveX control ins...
inetres/IZ_PolicyNotificationBarActiveXU.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyNotificationBarDownload.Desc:
This policy setting determines whether users will be prompted for non user-initiated file downloads. Regardless of this setting, users will receive file download dialogs for user-initiated downloads.

If you enable this setting, users will receive a file download dialog for automatic download attempts.

If you disable or do not configure this setting, file downloads that are not user-initiated will be blocked, and users will see the Notification bar instead of the file download dialog. Users ...
inetres/IZ_PolicyNotificationBarDownload.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyRunActiveXControls_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether ActiveX controls and plug-ins can be run on pages from the specified zone.

If you enable this policy setting, controls and plug-ins can run without user intervention.

If you selected Prompt in the drop-down box, users are asked to choose whether to allow the controls or plug-in to run.

If you disable this policy setting, controls and plug-ins are prevented from running.

If you do not configure this policy setting, controls and plug-ins are ...
inetres/IZ_PolicyRunActiveXControls_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptActiveXMarkedSafe.Friendly: Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting
inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptActiveXMarkedSafe.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether an ActiveX control marked safe for scripting can interact with a script.

If you enable this policy setting, script interaction can occur automatically without user intervention.

If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow script interaction.

If you disable this policy setting, script interaction is prevented from occurring.

If you do not configure this policy setting, script interaction is prevent...
inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptActiveXMarkedSafe.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyAntiMalwareCheckingOfAc.Friendly: Don't run antimalware programs against ActiveX controls
inetres/IZ_PolicyAntiMalwareCheckingOfAc.Desc:
This policy setting determines whether Internet Explorer runs antimalware programs against ActiveX controls, to check if they're safe to load on pages.

If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer won't check with your antimalware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control.

If you disable this policy setting, Internet Explorer always checks with your antimalware program to see if it's safe to create an instance of the ActiveX control.

If you don't conf...
inetres/IZ_PolicyAntiMalwareCheckingOfAc.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptActiveXNotMarkedS.Friendly: Initialize and script ActiveX controls not marked as safe
inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptActiveXNotMarkedS.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage ActiveX controls not marked as safe.

If you enable this policy setting, ActiveX controls are run, loaded with parameters, and scripted without setting object safety for untrusted data or scripts. This setting is not recommended, except for secure and administered zones. This setting causes both unsafe and safe controls to be initialized and scripted, ignoring the Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting option.

If you enable this policy sett...
inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptActiveXNotMarkedS.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptingOfJavaApplets_.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether applets are exposed to scripts within the zone.

If you enable this policy setting, scripts can access applets automatically without user intervention.

If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow scripts to access applets.

If you disable this policy setting, scripts are prevented from accessing applets.

If you do not configure this policy setting, scripts are prevented from accessing applets.
inetres/IZ_PolicyScriptingOfJavaApplets_.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicySignedFrameworkComponen.Friendly: Run .NET Framework-reliant components signed with Authenticode
inetres/IZ_PolicySignedFrameworkComponen.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether .NET Framework components that are signed with Authenticode can be executed from Internet Explorer. These components include managed controls referenced from an object tag and managed executables referenced from a link.

If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer will execute signed managed components. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, Internet Explorer will prompt the user to determine whether to execute signed managed compo...
inetres/IZ_PolicySignedFrameworkComponen.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicySoftwareChannelPermissi.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage software channel permissions.

If you enable this policy setting, you can choose the following options from the drop-down box.

Low safety to allow users to be notified of software updates by e-mail, software packages to be automatically downloaded to users' computers, and software packages to be automatically installed on users' computers.

Medium safety to allow users to be notified of software updates by e-mail and software packages to be automatica...
inetres/IZ_PolicySoftwareChannelPermissi.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicySubmitNonencryptedFormD.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether data on HTML forms on pages in the zone may be submitted. Forms sent with SSL (Secure Sockets Layer) encryption are always allowed; this setting only affects non-SSL form data submission.

If you enable this policy setting, information using HTML forms on pages in this zone can be submitted automatically. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, users are queried to choose whether to allow information using HTML forms on pages in this zone to...
inetres/IZ_PolicySubmitNonencryptedFormD.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyUnsignedFrameworkCompon.Friendly: Run .NET Framework-reliant components not signed with Authenticode
inetres/IZ_PolicyUnsignedFrameworkCompon.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether .NET Framework components that are not signed with Authenticode can be executed from Internet Explorer. These components include managed controls referenced from an object tag and managed executables referenced from a link.

If you enable this policy setting, Internet Explorer will execute unsigned managed components. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, Internet Explorer will prompt the user to determine whether to execute unsigned manag...
inetres/IZ_PolicyUnsignedFrameworkCompon.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyUserdataPersistence_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage the preservation of information in the browser's history, in favorites, in an XML store, or directly within a Web page saved to disk. When a user returns to a persisted page, the state of the page can be restored if this policy setting is appropriately configured.

If you enable this policy setting, users can preserve information in the browser's history, in favorites, in an XML store, or directly within a Web page saved to disk.

If you disable this p...
inetres/IZ_PolicyUserdataPersistence_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyWindowsRestrictionsURLa.Friendly: Allow script-initiated windows without size or position constraints
inetres/IZ_PolicyWindowsRestrictionsURLa.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage restrictions on script-initiated pop-up windows and windows that include the title and status bars.

If you enable this policy setting, Windows Restrictions security will not apply in this zone. The security zone runs without the added layer of security provided by this feature.

If you disable this policy setting, the possible harmful actions contained in script-initiated pop-up windows and windows that include the title and status bars cannot be run....
inetres/IZ_PolicyWindowsRestrictionsURLa.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyZoneElevationURLaction_.Friendly: Web sites in less privileged Web content zones can navigate into this zone
inetres/IZ_PolicyZoneElevationURLaction_.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether Web sites from less privileged zones, such as Internet sites, can navigate into this zone.

If you enable this policy setting, Web sites from less privileged zones can open new windows in, or navigate into, this zone. The security zone will run without the added layer of security that is provided by the Protection from Zone Elevation security feature. If you select Prompt in the drop-down box, a warning is issued to the user that potentially ri...
inetres/IZ_PolicyZoneElevationURLaction_.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowVBScript_8.Friendly: Allow VBScript to run in Internet Explorer
inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowVBScript_8.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to manage whether VBScript can be run on pages from the specified zone in Internet Explorer.

If you selected Enable in the drop-down box, VBScript can run without user intervention.

If you selected Prompt in the drop-down box, users are asked to choose whether to allow VBScript to run.

If you selected Disable in the drop-down box, VBScript is prevented from running.

If you do not configure or disable this policy setting, VBScript is prevented from running.
inetres/IZ_PolicyAllowVBScript_8.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page > Locked-Down Restricted Sites Zone

inetres/IZ_IncludeUnspecifiedLocalSites.Friendly: Intranet Sites: Include all local (intranet) sites not listed in other zones
inetres/IZ_IncludeUnspecifiedLocalSites.Desc:
This policy setting controls whether local sites which are not explicitly mapped into any Security Zone are forced into the local Intranet security zone.

If you enable this policy setting, local sites which are not explicitly mapped into a zone are considered to be in the Intranet Zone.

If you disable this policy setting, local sites which are not explicitly mapped into a zone will not be considered to be in the Intranet Zone (so would typically be in the Internet Zone).

If you do not conf...
inetres/IZ_IncludeUnspecifiedLocalSites.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page

inetres/IZ_PolicyWarnCertMismatch.Friendly: Turn on certificate address mismatch warning
inetres/IZ_PolicyWarnCertMismatch.Desc:
This policy setting allows you to turn on the certificate address mismatch security warning. When this policy setting is turned on, the user is warned when visiting Secure HTTP (HTTPS) websites that present certificates issued for a different website address. This warning helps prevent spoofing attacks.

If you enable this policy setting, the certificate address mismatch warning always appears.

If you disable or do not configure this policy setting, the user can choose whether the certificat...
inetres/IZ_PolicyWarnCertMismatch.Category: windows:WindowsComponents > Internet Explorer > Internet Control Panel > Security Page

inetres/IZ_PolicyInternetZoneLockdownTem.Desc:
This template policy setting allows you to configure policy settings in this zone consistent with a selected security level, for example, Low, Medium Low, Medium, or High.

If you enable this template policy setting and select a security level, all values for individual settings in the zone will be overwritten by the standard template defaults.

If you disable this template policy setting, no security level is configured.

If you do not configure this template policy setting, no security leve...
